pattern: Choosing which balloon to burst last in each subinterval decouples the two sides, so an interval dynamic program over padded boundaries finds the best total in cubic time.
hints: Bursting a balloon first is hard because it changes both neighbours; instead reason about which balloon in a range is burst last.
If balloon k is the last one popped within a range, its neighbours at that moment are the fixed walls just outside the range, and the two subranges become independent.
Pad the array with ones at both ends and fill a table over intervals: the value for a range is the max over k inside it of left[i..k] + right[k..j] plus nums[i-1]*nums[k]*nums[j+1].

edge_inputs.description: single balloon bursts against two implicit ones
a zero balloon should be popped to remove its dampening factor
all ones means every burst yields exactly one
descending values reward saving the largest for last

stress.description: moderate random array within the value range so the cubic DP stays fast
stress.note: size capped so the O(n^3) reference finishes quickly
